message:
Add alloc_batch to GraphemePool API

Implement alloc_batch() for batch allocation of multiple graphemes.
This method pre-allocates the result vector and allocates each grapheme
in sequence. Like alloc(), it does NOT deduplicate - each grapheme gets
its own slot.

Also includes comprehensive test coverage for:
- Empty batch allocation
- Single and multiple grapheme batches
- Duplicate handling (no deduplication)
- Width preservation
- Freed slot reuse

Closes: bd-2hnr

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-2hnr","title":"Implement alloc_batch(graphemes) - Batch allocation","description":"# Task: Implement alloc_batch()

## Function Signature (Rust)
```rust
pub fn alloc_batch(&mut self, graphemes: &[&str]) -> Vec<u32>
```

## Purpose
Allocates multiple graphemes atomically. More efficient than individual alloc() calls and ensures consistency.

## Implementation Notes
- Pre-allocate space if needed
- Allocate all in one operation
- Return IDs in same order as input
- Handle duplicates (share IDs? or separate?)

## Atomic Behavior
If allocation fails mid-batch, either:
1. Roll back all allocations
2. Or document partial failure behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Allocates all graphemes
- [ ] Returns IDs in order
- [ ] More efficient than individual calls
- [ ] Handles empty input

## Testing

 {"id":"bd-2ihx","title":"Implement Event Bus - Global event dispatcher","description":"# Task: Implement Event Bus

## Function Signatures (Rust)
```rust
pub type EventCallback = Box<dyn Fn(&str, &EventData) + Send + Sync>;

pub fn set_event_callback(callback: EventCallback);
pub fn emit_event(name: &str, data: EventData);
pub fn clear_event_callback();
```

## Purpose
Global event dispatcher for terminal responses and library events. Allows applications to receive async notifications.

## EventData Type
```rust
pub enum EventData {
    TerminalResponse { raw: Vec<u8>, parsed: Option<TerminalResponse> },
    CapabilityDetected { capability: String, supported: bool },
    Error { message: String },
    Custom { data: Box<dyn Any + Send + Sync> },
}
```

## Implementation Notes
1. Use OnceLock or lazy_static for global callback
2. Make callback optional (events no-op if not set)
3. Consider event queue for high-frequency events
4. Thread-safe callback invocation

## Events to Emit
- `terminal.response` - Raw terminal response received
- `terminal.capability` - Capability detected/confirmed
- `renderer.frame` - Frame rendered (with stats)
- `error` - Library error occurred

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EventCallback type defined
- [ ] EventData enum defined
- [ ] set_event_callback works
- [ ] emit_event invokes callback
- [ ] Thread-safe implementation

## Testing
- Set callback, emit event, verify received
- Emit without callback (no panic)
- Concurrent event emission","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:20:49.198110683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:20:49.204617255Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ihx","depends_on_id":"bd-1q0f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:20:49.204593661Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
